item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, is it a blast from the past or what ?
I wrote this SCP way back when the community was mostly 4chan's /x/, and seeing how big and impressive it grew over the years, it's a bit weird for me to think I wrote one below the hundred mark. I was just in the right place at the right time I guess.

075 isn't really anything to write home about and it was never meant to be, really. It came at a time where SCPs really were just meant to be strange artifacts or lifeforms that should never see the light of day, not centerpieces to an entire mythos. Not to denigrate the recent SCP efforts, there have been some true masterpieces along the years. But there is just something about the simpler, humbler times.

As such, I think 075 is perfectly fine as is. There is room to make it a keter, but I don't think it would be appropriate. Oh, and whoever came up with the idea that its corrosive compound should be caustic rather than acidic gets my full support. It makes it that little bit more bizarre, which counts for a lot in an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 to the little guy! I feel this one is a tad underappreciated. It holds up! Also has one of the more tasteful cross-CPs, which I'm totally OK with (and it's nice to get this article a little more traffic).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A question about the secretion substance: Does it have a vapor point, or has the foundation been able to pinpoint its triple point via heating or refrigeration? I figure since it's in the magic-cup-of-invincibility, the foundation could potentially attempt all sorts of atmospheric experiments on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be a funny one on SCP:CB like a little companion. But you need like special gloves or something.

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The image in this article is a non-CC image, created and ownded by Takeshi Yamada, and used here with their permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This manages to compress so much WTF into such a little space, and makes it all work towards making it more effective. I, for one, greatly enjoy this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On reread… this does nothing for me. It just isn't very interesting. -1'ed.

I do not, however, have any problem with the crosslink at the bottom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture definitely does sell this one. That is one seriously creepy snail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i would like this so much more without the cross-SCP reference at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With Paradox's help, I've rewritten the article slightly, specifically to make its secretions basic, rather than acidic. Bases can be equally corrosive as acids, and are naturally slimy, thus better fitting the subject matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Miss Lohner is correct. Though the article could be tweaked in a Keter direction if the author so chose, as it is, this creature is firmly Euclid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture does the same to me too… it freaking SCARES me man… where the heck did they come up with it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi far2,

With regards to the constant menace of SCP-682, would it be possible to replicate a small army of SCP-075 and turn them loose on SCP-682? It would seem that this bitter six toed mollusk would be a natural enemy of SCP-682.

Best Regards,
thekit
PhD candidate
Division of Consumer Technology Research

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always loved this one, the picture totally sells it.

Also, off-topic, the google ad under it made me laugh. "I CURED MY ACID REFLUX - refluxremedy.com"
